THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter launched a new Parks Committee with eyes on a $150,000–$200,000 playground overhaul at Bushka Park, while the Wausau School Board and its teachers union opened contract talks for the coming school year.

  • Kronenwetter's newly formed Parks Committee elected Erin Mishka chair and Dan Lesniac vice chair at its inaugural June 29 meeting, focusing immediately on a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park.
  • The Parks Committee scheduled a public parks tour for July 8 at 4 p.m. at the municipal center, with a July 9 rain date, and set a July 17 movie-under-the-stars fundraiser to support the Bushka Park project.
  • The Wausau Board of Education and the Wausau Education Association exchanged initial written contract proposals for the 2026-27 school year at a July 1 public negotiations session.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is set to vote on revisions to its Chapter 382 nuisances ordinance and consider traffic control measures on County Road XX and Terrebonne Drive at its July 6 meeting.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Weston's Village Board and Community Life & Public Safety Committee and Wausau's Parks & Recreation Committee, all meeting July 6. Later in the week, Marathon County's Infrastructure Committee meets Thursday, July 9, alongside Wausau's Infrastructure & Facilities and Sustainability, Energy & Environment committees.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
